title: KPop Demon Hunters Wins Oscar for Best Animated Feature 2026
text: Netflix and Sony Pictures Animation's KPop Demon Hunters won the Academy Award for Best Animated Feature Film at the 2026 Oscars ceremony on March 15. The film's writer-directors Maggie Kang and Chris Appelhans, along with producer Michelle L. M. Wong, accepted the award. The film has become Netflix's most-watched film in history, accumulating over 500 million views. It was also nominated for Original Song for Golden, which topped the Billboard 200 and became the highest-charting soundtrack of 2025. Golden is the first K-pop album to simultaneously top U.S. Apple Music and iTunes charts. Kang emphasized the significance of Korean cultural representation, stating: I'm so proud of Korean film and movies about Korea and expressed her hope to see more films, especially in animation that focus on different cultures. The film follows three K-pop stars in the group Huntr/x who use their ancient demon-slayer lineage to battle supernatural threats while confronting an evil demon-backed boy band.
